topic: Someone finds a wallet containing a large sum of cash
raw_output:
[story 1]

The leather of the wallet felt strangely heavy in my palm. It was thick, expensive-looking, and when I flipped it open, the sight of the bills inside made my breath catch. A substantial stack of cash, far more than I expected, spilled across the lining. My fingers hovered over the money, then slowly retracted, as if the paper itself held some kind of static charge. I stood there by the curb, the afternoon sun glinting off the edges of the bills, my gaze darting around the quiet street. Every passing car seemed to slow down, and I found myself counting the denominations in my head, a nervous rhythm taking over my thoughts. I tucked the wallet deep into my jacket pocket, the fabric suddenly feeling too tight across my chest.

[story 2]

I found the abandoned leather purse tucked beneath a park bench. When I opened it, the sheer volume of currency inside hit me like a physical wave. It was an astonishing amount of money, crisp and new. A cold prickle ran down my spine as I examined the contents. My hands started to tremble slightly as I tried to organize the bundles, the weight of the cash pressing down on my palms. I quickly closed the wallet, my movements becoming jerky. I walked away from the bench quickly, my pace too fast, my eyes constantly sweeping the surrounding trees and shadows, half-expecting someone to materialize from the foliage.

[story 3]

It was just a forgotten item, a wallet lying on the sidewalk near the entrance of the library. But when I saw the contents—a significant wad of cash—a sudden tension seized my shoulders. I knelt down, my movements slow and deliberate, as if touching the money might somehow alter its nature. The paper felt slick under my fingertips. I picked up a single bill, turning it over and over, my jaw tightening. A strange hollowness settled in my stomach, and I stood up abruptly, stuffing the wallet into my bag with unnecessary force, eager to be anywhere else.
